items.title: India's Marine Food Export Reaches Record High of Over ₹72,000 Crore
items.whyInNews: India's marine food export has reached a record high of over ₹72,000 crore, with frozen shrimp being the leading export item. The United States and China are the top importers of Indian marine products.
items.summary: The export of marine food products from India has reached an all-time high of over ₹72,000 crore. Frozen shrimp is the leading export item, with the United States and China being the top importers. This achievement is a significant milestone in the history of marine food exports from India.
keyPoints: India's marine food export has reached ₹72,000 crore
Frozen shrimp is the leading export item
The United States and China are the top importers
This is the highest export figure in the history of Indian marine food exports
importantPoints: The Marine Products Export Development Authority (MPEDA) was established in 1972 and is headquartered in Kochi
The Export-Import Bank of India was established under the Export-Import Bank of India Act, 1981
India's marine food export has been increasing steadily over the years, with a growth rate of 10% in the last fiscal year
India is the second-largest exporter of marine products in the world, with a market share of 10%
The increase in marine food export is expected to boost the Indian economy and create more employment opportunities
items.examRelevance: Economy: Foreign Trade and Balance of Payments

items.title: India and South Korea Sign MoU to Strengthen Cooperation in MSME Sector
items.whyInNews: India and South Korea have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen cooperation in the Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector. The MoU aims to promote mutual cooperation and exchange of information between the two countries.
items.summary: The MoU was signed between the Ministry of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises of India and the Ministry of Trade, Industry, and Energy of South Korea. The agreement aims to promote cooperation in areas such as technology transfer, joint research, and development of MSMEs.
keyPoints: India and South Korea have signed an MoU to strengthen cooperation in the MSME sector
The MoU aims to promote mutual cooperation and exchange of information
The agreement will promote cooperation in areas such as technology transfer and joint research
The MoU will help to increase trade and investment between the two countries
importantPoints: The Ministry of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises was established in 2007 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises Development Act, 2006, provides for the promotion and development of MSMEs
India and South Korea have a long history of cooperation in the MSME sector, with several agreements and MoUs signed in the past
The MoU is expected to increase trade and investment between the two countries and promote economic growth
The agreement will also help to promote innovation and entrepreneurship in the MSME sector

items.title: National Mission for Clean Ganga Achieves Significant Milestones in 2025-26
items.whyInNews: The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) has achieved significant milestones in the fiscal year 2025-26, including the completion of several infrastructure projects and the increase in the capacity of sewage treatment plants.
items.summary: The NMCG has achieved significant milestones in the fiscal year 2025-26, including the completion of several infrastructure projects and the increase in the capacity of sewage treatment plants. The mission has also launched several initiatives to promote sustainable development and conservation of the Ganga river.
keyPoints: The NMCG has achieved significant milestones in the fiscal year 2025-26
Several infrastructure projects have been completed, including sewage treatment plants
The capacity of sewage treatment plants has been increased
The mission has launched several initiatives to promote sustainable development and conservation of the Ganga river
importantPoints: The National Mission for Clean Ganga was established in 2014 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The National Ganga River Basin Authority was established under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986
The NMCG has launched several initiatives to promote sustainable development and conservation of the Ganga river, including the Namami Gange programme
The mission has achieved significant progress in the past few years, with several projects completed and several more in the pipeline
The NMCG is working to promote sustainable development and conservation of the Ganga river and its tributaries

items.title: India and Bhutan Hold 7th Joint Group on Customs Meeting
items.whyInNews: India and Bhutan have held the 7th Joint Group on Customs meeting in Munnar, Kerala, to discuss customs cooperation and trade facilitation between the two countries.
items.summary: The meeting was held from April 20-21, 2026, and was attended by officials from the customs departments of both countries. The meeting discussed various issues related to customs cooperation and trade facilitation, including the simplification of customs procedures and the reduction of trade barriers.
keyPoints: India and Bhutan have held the 7th Joint Group on Customs meeting
The meeting was held in Munnar, Kerala, from April 20-21, 2026
The meeting discussed customs cooperation and trade facilitation between the two countries
The meeting aimed to simplify customs procedures and reduce trade barriers
importantPoints: The India-Bhutan Friendship Treaty was signed in 1949 and provides for cooperation in various areas, including trade and commerce
The Customs Act, 1962, provides for the regulation of customs in India
India and Bhutan have a long history of cooperation in the area of customs and trade facilitation
The meeting is expected to promote trade and economic cooperation between the two countries
The meeting will also help to strengthen the bilateral relationship between India and Bhutan

items.title: Indian Navy's INS Sunayana Reaches Jakarta to Strengthen Maritime Ties with Indonesia
items.whyInNews: The Indian Navy's INS Sunayana has reached Jakarta, Indonesia, as part of the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) outreach programme to strengthen maritime ties with Indonesia.
items.summary: The INS Sunayana is on a deployment to the IOR as part of the Indian Navy's efforts to strengthen maritime ties with countries in the region. The ship's visit to Jakarta is aimed at promoting bilateral cooperation and friendship between India and Indonesia.
keyPoints: The INS Sunayana has reached Jakarta, Indonesia, as part of the IOR outreach programme
The ship's visit aims to promote bilateral cooperation and friendship between India and Indonesia
The INS Sunayana is on a deployment to the IOR to strengthen maritime ties with countries in the region
The visit is expected to promote mutual understanding and cooperation between the two navies
importantPoints: The Indian Navy was established in 1950 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The Indian Navy has a long history of cooperation with the Indonesian Navy, with several joint exercises and training programmes
The INS Sunayana is a part of the Indian Navy's Eastern Fleet and is based in Visakhapatnam
The ship's visit to Jakarta is expected to promote trade and economic cooperation between India and Indonesia
The visit will also help to strengthen the bilateral relationship between India and Indonesia

items.title: Iran to Restart Domestic Flights
items.whyInNews: Iran has announced plans to restart domestic flights and reopen its airspace in phases, providing relief to international airlines connecting Europe with Asia, including Indian carriers. The decision was made by the Iranian government.
items.summary: Iran's government has decided to restart domestic flights and reopen its airspace in phases. This move is expected to bring huge relief to all international airlines connecting Europe with Asia, including Indian carriers. The Iranian government has planned to first reallow overflight operations.
keyPoints: Iran to restart domestic flights
Iran to reopen its airspace in phases
Relief to international airlines connecting Europe with Asia
Indian carriers to benefit from the decision
importantPoints: International Air Transport Association (IATA) - full form, founded in 1945, headquarters in Montreal
Related to the Chicago Convention on International Civil Aviation, 1944
Historical context: Iran's airspace was closed due to the COVID-19 pandemic
Key statistic: Over 100 international airlines operate flights between Europe and Asia
Policy implication: The decision will boost air travel and trade between Europe and Asia

items.title: Premier League: Brighton Outclass Chelsea
items.whyInNews: Brighton has outclassed Chelsea in a Premier League match, winning 3-0. The result sees Brighton climb above Chelsea to sixth in the table on 50 points.
items.summary: Brighton has defeated Chelsea 3-0 in a Premier League match. The win takes Brighton above Chelsea to sixth in the table on 50 points, two ahead of Chelsea. Brighton's victory is a significant upset in the Premier League.
keyPoints: Brighton defeats Chelsea 3-0
Brighton climbs to sixth in the Premier League table
Chelsea drops to seventh in the table
Brighton has 50 points, two ahead of Chelsea

Policy implication: The win boosts Brighton's chances of qualifying for European competitions

items.title: IPL 2026: Abhishek and Malinga Star in SRH's Win
items.whyInNews: Abhishek and Malinga have starred in Sunrisers Hyderabad's (SRH) win against Delhi Capitals (DC) in the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026. Abhishek scored an unbeaten 135, while Malinga took four wickets.

items.title: Aronyak Becomes a Grandmaster
items.whyInNews: Aronyak has become a Grandmaster, achieving the highest title in chess. He is one of the youngest players to achieve this feat.
items.summary: Aronyak has achieved the title of Grandmaster, the highest title in chess. He is one of the youngest players to become a Grandmaster. Aronyak's achievement is a significant milestone in Indian chess.

Aronyak is one of the youngest Grandmasters
